title: Your First Script
objective:
Write `solution.sh` so it prints exactly `LEVEL 1: Your First Script | <arg1> | <arg2>`.
Use the script arguments as `<arg1>` and `<arg2>`, preserving spaces exactly.
Exit with status 0 on success.

test_cases.expected_stdout: LEVEL 1: Your First Script | alpha | beta
LEVEL 1: Your First Script | spaces allowed | 42

title: First Argument
objective: Print only the first positional argument. Use `$1`, quote it, and do not print any other arguments.

title: Multiple Arguments
objective: Print the first three positional arguments in the format `first=<arg1> second=<arg2> third=<arg3>`. Use `$1`, `$2`, and `$3` directly, with quotes around every expansion.

title: Argument Count
objective: Print `count: N`, where `N` is the number of arguments passed to the script. Use `$#`.

title: All Arguments
objective: Loop over all positional arguments safely with `"$@"` and print each one on its own numbered line as `N: <arg>`. Inputs containing spaces must stay one argument.

title: Shift Arguments
objective: Use `shift` once to discard the first argument, then print the new `$1` as `next: <arg>`. This shows how arguments move left after shifting.

title: Require an Argument
objective: Require at least one argument. If none is provided, print nothing and exit with status 1. Otherwise print `arg: <first-arg>`.

title: Default Argument
objective: Print `Hello, <name>` where `<name>` is the first argument if provided, otherwise `guest`. Use `${1:-guest}` rather than an `if` statement.

title: Read from Stdin
objective: Read one line from the file path in `$1` using `read -r value < "$1"`, then print `You said: <value>`. This practices the same `read` pattern used for stdin while keeping tests non-interactive.

title: Read with Prompt
objective: Use `read -r -p` to read a name from the file path in `$1`, then print `Name is: <value>`. The prompt itself is not part of the expected output in this non-interactive test.

title: Read Silently
objective: Use `read -r -s` to read a password from the file path in `$1`, then print only `Password length: N`, where `N` is the number of characters. Do not print the password.

title: Read with Timeout
objective: Use `read -r -t 1` to read from the file path in `$1`. If the read succeeds, print `read: <value>`; if it times out or fails, print `timeout` and exit with status 1.

title: Read into Array
objective: Use `read -r -a words` to split the first line of the file path in `$1` into an array. Print `count=<n> first=<first> last=<last>`.

title: Validate Numeric Input
objective: Validate that the first argument contains only digits using `[[ $value =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]`. If valid, print `number: <value>`; otherwise print nothing and exit with status 1.

title: Validate Non-Empty
objective: Validate that the first argument is not empty. If it is empty or missing, print nothing and exit with status 1. Otherwise print `value: <arg>`.

title: Read a File Line by Line
objective: Read the file path in `$1` line by line with `while IFS= read -r line`. Print each line with a one-based line number as `N: <line>`.

title: Read from Pipe
objective: Detect whether stdin is a pipe with `[ -p /dev/stdin ]`. With piped input, count lines and print `pipe: N lines`; without piped input, print `no pipe`.

title: Handle Quoted Arguments
objective: Loop over `"$@"` and print each argument on its own numbered line as `argN=<value>`. Quoted arguments containing spaces must remain intact.

title: Named Flags (manual)
objective: Manually parse `--name VALUE` and `--count VALUE` using a `while` loop, `case`, and `shift`. Print `name=<name> count=<count>`. Default missing values to `unknown` and `0`.

title: Getopts Basics
objective: Use `getopts "ab:" opt` to parse `-a` as a boolean flag and `-b VALUE` as an option with an argument. Print `a=<true|false> b=<value>`, defaulting to `a=false b=none`.

title: Getopts with Required Option
objective: Use `getopts` to require `-f VALUE`. If `-f` is missing or has no value, print nothing and exit with status 1. If present, print `file=<value>`.

title: Simple if Statement
objective: Use a simple `if [ condition ]; then` statement. If the first argument is `yes`, print `matched`; otherwise print nothing and exit 0.

title: if-else
objective: Use `if`/`else` to make a binary decision. Print `enabled` when the first argument is `on`; otherwise print `disabled`.

title: if-elif-else
objective: Use `if`/`elif`/`else` for three branches. Print `starting` for `start`, `stopping` for `stop`, and `unknown` for anything else.

title: Test String Equality
objective: Compare the first two arguments as strings with `[ "$a" = "$b" ]`. Print `same` when they match, otherwise `different`.

title: Test String Inequality
objective: Compare the first two arguments as strings with `!=`. Print `different` when they do not match, otherwise `same`.

title: Test Empty String
objective: Use `-z` to test whether the first argument is empty. Print `empty` for an empty string or missing argument, otherwise print `not empty`.

title: Test Non-Empty String
objective: Use `-n` to test whether the first argument is non-empty. Print `present` when it has text, otherwise print `missing`.

title: Numeric Comparison
objective: Compare the first two arguments as integers. Print `less`, `equal`, or `greater` using numeric test operators such as `-lt`, `-eq`, and `-gt`.

title: Double Bracket Test
objective: Use `[[ ... ]]` to test whether the first argument ends in `.txt`. Print `text file` for `.txt` names, otherwise `other`.

title: Logical AND in Test
objective: Use `&&` inside `[[ ]]` to check that both the first and second arguments are non-empty. Print `complete` only when both are present; otherwise print `incomplete`.

title: Logical OR in Test
objective: Use `||` inside `[[ ]]` to check whether either of the first two arguments is `yes`. Print `allowed` if either one is `yes`; otherwise print `denied`.

title: Negate a Condition
objective: Use `!` to negate a condition. Print `guest` when the first argument is not `admin`; print `admin` when it is exactly `admin`.

title: File Exists
objective: Inspect the path in `$1`. Print `file` if it is a regular file, `directory` if it is a directory, and `missing` if it does not exist.

title: File is Readable
objective: Use `-r` to test whether the path in `$1` is readable. Print `readable` if it is readable, otherwise `not readable`.

title: File is Not Empty
objective: Use `-s` to test whether the path in `$1` exists and has a size greater than zero. Print `not empty` or `empty`.

title: Compare File Ages
objective: Create two temporary files, make the second one newer, and compare them with `-nt`. Print exactly `second newer`.

title: Case Statement
objective: Use a `case` statement on the first argument. Print `red=#ff0000`, `green=#00ff00`, `blue=#0000ff`, or `unknown`.

title: Case with Glob Patterns
objective: Use `case` glob patterns to classify the first argument. Print `shell` for `*.sh`, `text` for `*.txt`, and `other` for everything else.

title: Ternary-Style One-Liner
objective: Use a conditional one-liner with `[[ ... ]] && ... || ...`. Print `pass` when the first argument is `ok`; otherwise print `fail`.

title: Check Command Exists
objective: Use `command -v` to check whether the command named in `$1` exists. Print `found` if it does, otherwise `missing`.

title: Check OS Type
objective: Inspect `uname -s` and print exactly one of `Linux`, `macOS`, or `Other`.

title: Nested if Statements
objective: Use nested `if` statements to classify two arguments: role and environment. Print `admin-prod`, `admin-other`, or `user`.

title: for Loop over List
objective: Use `for item in alpha beta gamma` to print each word on its own line.

title: for Loop over Range
objective: Use brace expansion in a `for` loop to print the numbers 1 through 5, one per line.

title: C-Style for Loop
objective: Use a C-style `for` loop to print `index=0`, `index=1`, and `index=2`.

title: while Loop
objective: Use a `while [ condition ]` loop to print `count=1`, `count=2`, and `count=3`.

title: until Loop
objective: Use an `until [ condition ]` loop to print `count=1`, `count=2`, and `count=3`.

title: Infinite Loop with Break
objective: Use `while true` with `break` to print `tick=1`, `tick=2`, and `done`, then stop.

title: Continue in Loop
objective: Loop over the numbers 1 through 5 and use `continue` to skip 3. Print the remaining numbers, one per line.

title: Loop over Arguments
objective: Loop over all command-line arguments with `for arg in "$@"` and print each as `arg=<value>`.

title: Loop over Files
objective: Loop over `fixtures/*.txt` and print each matching basename. Ignore non-text files.

title: Loop over Lines of a File
objective: Read the file path in `$1` line by line with `while IFS= read -r line` and print each line as `line=<value>`.

title: Loop with Counter
objective: Read the file path in `$1` and print each line with a one-based counter as `N:<line>`.

title: Nested Loops
objective: Use nested loops to print all row/column pairs for rows `A B` and columns `1 2` in row-major order.

title: Loop with Index
objective: Store `red green blue` in an array, then use a C-style index loop to print `0:red`, `1:green`, and `2:blue`.

title: Loop until Input Valid
objective: Loop over the arguments until you find the first all-numeric value. Print `valid=<value>` and stop. If none is found, print `no valid input`.

title: Accumulate in Loop
objective: Loop over all numeric arguments, accumulate their sum, and print `sum=<total>`.

title: Loop with Pipe
objective: Read piped input with a `while read` loop and count the lines. With piped input, print `piped=<count>`; without piped input, print `piped=0`.

title: Select Menu
objective: Use the `select` built-in with options `apple`, `banana`, and `cherry`. Read the numeric choice from `$1` non-interactively and print `selected=<option>`.

title: Retry with Backoff
objective: Simulate three retry attempts with exponential backoff values. Print `attempt=1 wait=1`, `attempt=2 wait=2`, and `attempt=3 wait=4`.

test_cases.expected_stdout:
attempt=1 wait=1
attempt=2 wait=2
attempt=3 wait=4

title: Loop over Command Output
objective: Use process substitution to loop over command output from `printf`. Print each generated word as `cmd=<word>`.

title: Generate a Sequence
objective: Use `seq` in a loop to print `num=1` through `num=4`.

title: Loop Early Exit Pattern
objective: Read the file path in `$1` and print lines until a line equals `STOP`. Stop immediately and do not print `STOP` or later lines.

title: Parallel Loop Jobs
objective: Start three background jobs in a loop with `&`, wait for them with `wait`, then print `job 1 done`, `job 2 done`, and `job 3 done` in order.

test_cases.expected_stdout:
job 1 done
job 2 done
job 3 done

title: Define a Function
objective: Define a function named `greet` that prints `hello from function`, then call it once.

title: Call a Function
objective: Define a function named `status` and invoke it twice so the script prints `ready` on two lines.

title: Function Arguments
objective: Define a function that reads its own `$1` and `$2`, then call it with the first two script arguments. Print `<first> -> <second>`.

title: Return Exit Code
objective: Define a function that returns success for `ok` and failure for anything else. Print `success` or `failure` based on the function return code.

title: Capture Return Value
objective: Call a function that returns exit code 7, capture `$?`, and print `code=7`.

title: Return a String
objective: Create a function that prints a string, capture it with command substitution, and print `value=<captured>`.

title: Local Variables
objective: Use `local` inside a function so changing `name` inside the function does not change the global `name`. Print `inside=local` and `outside=global`.

title: Default Argument in Function
objective: Define a function that uses `${1:-guest}` for its argument and prints `Hello, <name>`.

title: Validate Arguments in Function
objective: Write a function that requires one non-empty argument. If missing, print nothing and exit 1; otherwise print `valid=<value>`.

title: Function Library
objective: Source `fixtures/helpers.sh` and call its `say_library` function so the script prints `from library`.

title: Recursive Function
objective: Write a recursive `factorial` function and print `factorial(5)=120`.

title: Recursive Tree Walk
objective: Recursively walk `fixtures/tree` and print each file path relative to that directory in sorted traversal order.

title: Function as Callback
objective: Pass a function name as a callback to another function. Use it to print `HELLO`.

title: Functions in a Loop
objective: Define a function that formats one item, then call it from a loop over `build test deploy`.

title: Overriding a Function
objective: Define a function named `message`, call it, redefine it, then call it again. Print `old` then `new`.

title: Anonymous-Style Function
objective: Use an inline function definition inside a subshell or grouped block and print `inline`.

title: Error-Handling Function
objective: Define a `die` function that prints `error: missing value` to stderr and exits 1 when no argument is provided. With an argument, print `value=<arg>`.

title: Log Function
objective: Define a log function that prefixes a message with today in `YYYY-MM-DD` format. Print a line matching `[YYYY-MM-DD] deploy`.

title: Function with Array Arg
objective: Pass an array to a function using a nameref and print `count=3 first=red last=blue`.

title: Function Return Array
objective: Make a function output three values, capture them into an array with `mapfile`, and print `values=alpha,beta,gamma`.

title: String Length
objective: Print the length of the first argument as `length=N` using `${#str}`.

title: Substring
objective: Print five characters from the first argument starting at offset 2 using `${str:2:5}`.

title: Substring from End
objective: Print the last three characters of the first argument using a negative substring offset.

title: Strip Shortest Prefix
objective: Remove the shortest prefix through the first `/` from the first argument using `${str#*/}`.

title: Strip Longest Prefix
objective: Remove the longest prefix through the last `/` from the first argument using `${str##*/}`.

title: Strip Shortest Suffix
objective: Remove the shortest suffix from the final `.` onward using `${str%.*}`.

title: Strip Longest Suffix
objective: Remove the longest suffix from the first `.` onward using `${str%%.*}`.

title: Replace First Match
objective: Replace only the first `-` in the first argument with `_` using `${str/-/_}`.

title: Replace All Matches
objective: Replace every `-` in the first argument with `_` using `${str//-/_}`.

title: Uppercase All
objective: Uppercase the entire first argument using `${str^^}`.

title: Lowercase All
objective: Lowercase the entire first argument using `${str,,}`.

title: Capitalize First Letter
objective: Capitalize only the first character of the first argument using `${str^}`.

title: Trim Leading Whitespace
objective: Trim leading spaces and tabs from the first argument using Bash parameter expansion.

title: Trim Trailing Whitespace
objective: Trim trailing spaces and tabs from the first argument using Bash parameter expansion.

title: Trim Both Ends
objective: Trim leading and trailing whitespace from the first argument.

title: Split String to Array
objective: Split the first argument on commas into an array and print `count=N first=<first> last=<last>`.

title: Join Array to String
objective: Join the arguments with commas using the `IFS=,` array join trick.

title: Check Prefix
objective: Print `yes` if the first argument starts with `pre`, otherwise print `no`.

title: Check Suffix
objective: Print `yes` if the first argument ends with `.log`, otherwise print `no`.

title: Check Substring
objective: Print `yes` if the first argument contains `mid`, otherwise print `no`.

title: Regex Match
objective: Use a regex to check whether the first argument is exactly three digits. Print `match` or `no match`.

title: Extract Regex Group
objective: Extract the name from `user:<name>` using a regex capture group and print `name=<name>`. If it does not match, print `no match`.

title: Integer Addition
objective: Add the first two integer arguments with arithmetic expansion and print `sum=<result>`.

title: Subtraction & Multiplication
objective: Use arithmetic expansion to subtract and multiply the first two integer arguments. Print `diff=<a-b> product=<a*b>`.

title: Division & Modulo
objective: Use integer division and modulo on the first two arguments. Print `quotient=<a/b> remainder=<a%b>`.

title: Power
objective: Raise the first integer argument to the power of the second using `**`. Print `power=<result>`.

title: Increment / Decrement
objective: Start with the first integer argument, increment once, decrement twice, then print `value=<result>`.

title: Compound Assignment
objective: Start with the first integer argument, add 5 with compound assignment, multiply by 2 with compound assignment, then print `value=<result>`.

title: let Command
objective: Use `let` to multiply the first two integer arguments and print `result=<product>`.

title: Arithmetic Condition
objective: Use an arithmetic condition to compare the first two integer arguments. Print `greater` if the first is greater, otherwise `not greater`.

title: Float Arithmetic with bc
objective: Use `bc` to divide the first number by the second with scale 2. Print `result=<value>`.

title: Random Number
objective: Use `$RANDOM` and print `random=<number>`. The output must be a valid integer.

title: Random in Range
objective: Generate a random integer from 0 through 9 using modulo and print `n=<value>`.

title: Convert Bases
objective: Convert the first decimal integer argument to lowercase hexadecimal with `printf` and print `hex=<value>`.

title: Sum a List
objective: Loop over all integer arguments, accumulate their sum, and print `sum=<total>`.

title: Min and Max
objective: Loop over integer arguments to find the minimum and maximum. Print `min=<min> max=<max>`.

title: Declare an Array
objective: Declare an indexed array containing alpha, beta, and gamma, then print all values on one line separated by spaces.

title: Access Element
objective: Create an array of colors and print only the first element using array index 0.

title: All Elements
objective: Print every array element on its own line, preserving an element that contains a space.

title: Array Length
objective: Store four server names in an array and print the number of elements as count=<number>.

title: Append to Array
objective: Start with two deployment stages, append production, and print the final stage list.

title: Remove Element
objective: Remove the middle element from an array with unset, then print the remaining values on separate lines.

title: Slice an Array
objective: Use array slicing to print three elements starting at index 1 from a five-item array.

title: Loop over Array
objective: Loop over an array of workflow steps and print each as step=<name>.

title: Loop with Index
objective: Loop over array indexes and print each index next to its value.

title: Array from Command
objective: Capture command output into an array with mapfile, then print the captured lines.

title: Array from File Lines
objective: Read the provided file into an array with mapfile, then print the line count, first line, and last line.

title: Search in Array
objective: Search a small array for the argument and print found when it is present, otherwise missing.

title: Sort an Array
objective: Sort an array alphabetically by piping its elements through sort and reading the result into a new array.

title: Unique Elements
objective: Print each unique array element once by combining sort and uniq.

title: Associative Array
objective: Declare an associative array of country capitals and print two known keys.

title: Add Key-Value
objective: Add two key-value pairs to an associative array and print both stored values.

title: Iterate Assoc Array
objective: Iterate over an associative array by key and print key=value pairs in sorted key order.

title: Check Key Exists
objective: Check whether the requested color key exists in an associative array and print exists or missing.

title: Delete Key
objective: Delete one key from an associative array, then prove the deleted key is gone and another key remains.

title: Pass Array to Function
objective: Pass an array to a function by name using a nameref, then print its count and first value.

title: Return Array from Function
objective: Make a function output one item per line, capture that output into an array, and print the joined result.

title: 2D Array Simulation
objective: Simulate a 2D array with associative-array keys like row,column and print a two-row grid.

title: Redirect stdout to File
objective: Redirect stdout into a file, append another line, then print the file contents.

title: Redirect stderr to File
objective: Send an error message to stderr, redirect it into a file, then print the captured error.

title: Redirect Both
objective: Redirect stdout and stderr from the same block into one file, then print that file.

title: Redirect stderr to stdout
objective: Redirect stderr into stdout so a pipeline can transform the error message.

title: Discard Output
objective: Discard noisy stdout with /dev/null and print only the visible status line.

title: Read from File with Redirect
objective: Read the first line from the provided file using input redirection and print it as first=<line>.

title: Here-Document
objective: Use a here-document to write two lines to a file, then print the file.

title: Here-Document No Expand
objective: Use a quoted here-document delimiter so variables remain literal text.

title: Here-String
objective: Feed a string into a command with a here-string and print the uppercase result.

title: Pipe Between Commands
objective: Pipe generated lines into grep and count only lines that contain beta.

title: Pipeline Exit Code
objective: Run a pipeline and print the exit status of each command with PIPESTATUS.

title: Process Substitution Input
objective: Use process substitution to compare two generated streams and print whether they match.

title: Process Substitution Output
objective: Send output through process substitution to transform it into a file, then print the transformed file.

title: Tee in Pipeline
objective: Use tee in a pipeline to log lines while still passing them to a counting command.

title: Custom File Descriptor
objective: Open file descriptor 3 for writing, write two lines through it, then print the file.

title: Close File Descriptor
objective: Open and close a custom file descriptor, then report that the descriptor was closed.

title: Redirect in Function
objective: Write function output to a log by redirecting the function call, then print the log.

title: Log Everything with exec
objective: Use exec with tee so later stdout is printed and logged, then show the log contents after restoring stdout.

build started
build finished

title: Check Exit Code

objective: Use a command followed by `|| exit 1` before printing the success message.

objective: Use `set -o pipefail` so a failing command inside a pipeline makes the pipeline fail.

objective: Use an EXIT trap to print a cleanup marker after the main work finishes.

objective: Use an ERR trap in a subshell to log that a command failed.

objective: Install a SIGINT trap and trigger it in a subshell to print a graceful message.

objective: Install a SIGTERM trap and trigger it in a subshell to print a graceful message.

objective: Create a temp file, remove it from an EXIT trap, and report that cleanup happened.

objective: Define a `die` function and use it to print one consistent error message.

objective: Use an early guard clause to reject an empty argument before doing the main work.

objective: Use `timeout` to stop a slow command and print the timeout exit code.

objective: Check for a required command with `command -v` before using it.

objective: Validate that the file argument exists and is readable before counting its lines.

objective: Return a command status from a function and report the propagated code.

objective: Use regex quantifiers to validate a two-letter, four-digit code.

objective: Use a sed capture group to wrap a key-value line in brackets.

objective: Create a file, append a second line with `>>`, then print the file.

objective: Read a file line by line and number each line.
